name: multiple_aggregate_comparison
description: A rule with two assignment aggregates exposes both grounded counts, their comparison, and every contributing selected literal without duplicates.
program:
b(X) | c(X) :- a(X).
maggiori_b :- #count{X: b(X)} = V1, #count{X: c(X)} = V2, V1 > V2.
maggiori_c :- #count{X: b(X)} = V1, #count{X: c(X)} = V2, V1 < V2.
uguali :- #count{X: b(X)} = V1, #count{X: c(X)} = V2, V1 = V2.
a(1..3).

expected_responses.rule: maggiori_c :- #count{X: b(X)} = V1, #count{X: c(X)} = V2, V1 < V2.

aggregate_expansions.rule: maggiori_c :- #count{X: b(X)} = V1, #count{X: c(X)} = V2, V1 < V2.

expected_truth.#count{X: b(X)} = 1: the aggregate condition is true and contributes to the result; expand to see how
expected_truth.#count{X: c(X)} = 2: the aggregate condition is true and contributes to the result; expand to see how
